rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to ports or features with visible names.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees; avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponents in type contracts.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure that all expressions use compatible types (e.g., bool for comparisons, int or real for numeric bounds).
Only include assumptions or guarantees that are directly supported by the requirement and AADL model structure.
Use named constants for numeric thresholds in AGREE contracts to improve clarity and maintainability.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and placed within component type or implementation scopes as appropriate.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference visible variables or ports; avoid using opaque or non-visible identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not assume or guarantee bounds for numeric ports unless explicitly stated in the requirement or clearly defined in the AADL model.
Avoid creating synthetic constraints like x >= 0 unless the requirement explicitly mandates non-negative values.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations such as Compute_Execution_Time, Deadline, Period, Priority, Dispatch_Protocol, Stack_Size, Code_Size, or unit tokens like ms/us/Hz/Bytes
Implementation-level bindings or assignments that are not valid in component type scopes
Boolean data range comparisons over opaque or enumeration-like data ports
